---
title: 'Random Study of Psalms 103'
date: '2024-10-18T06:50:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: false
categories:
  - 'NoHiding Faith Studies'
---
~~~{=html}
<div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div></div><div><br /></div><div><a href="https://ref.ly/logosres/leb?ref=BibleLEB2.Ps103.1" target="_blank"></a></div><blockquote><h4 style="text-align: left;"><a href="https://ref.ly/logosres/leb?ref=BibleLEB2.Ps103.1" target="_blank">Psalms 103</a>&nbsp;“<span style="font-style: italic;">Of David.</span></h4><div><p class="MsoNormal"><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">Bless Yahweh, O my soul, and all within me, bless his holy name.&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic;">Bless Yahweh, O my soul, and do not forget all his benefits:</span></p>
	<p class="MsoNormal"></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="font-style: italic;">who forgives (</span>סָלַח sālaḥ<span style="font-style: italic;">) all your </span><span style="font-style: italic;">iniquity (<span style="font-style: normal;">עָוֹן ʿāwōn</span>)</span><span style="font-style: italic;">,</span></li><li><span style="font-style: italic;">who heals (<span style="font-style: normal;">רָפָא rāpāʾ</span>) all your diseases (<span style="font-style: normal;">תַּחֲלֻאִים taḥaluʾîm</span>),</span></li><li><span style="font-style: italic;">who redeems (<span style="font-style: normal;">גְּאוּלִים geʾûlîm</span>) your life from the pit,</span>&nbsp;</li><li><i>who crowns you with loyal love and mercies,</i></li><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">who satisfies your life with good so that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.</span></li></ul></div></blockquote><blockquote><p></p><p class="MsoNormal"><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">Yahweh does deeds of justice and judgments for all who are oppressed,&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic;">who made known his ways to Moses, his deeds to the people of Israel.</span></p>
	<p class="MsoNormal"><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">Yahweh is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger and abundant in loyal love. </span>
	</p>
	<p class="MsoNormal"></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">He does not dispute continually, nor keep his anger forever. </span></li><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor repaid us according to our iniquities. </span></li></ul><p></p>
	
	<p class="MsoNormal"><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">For as the heavens are high above the earth, so his loyal love prevails over those who fear him. </span>
	</p>
	<p class="MsoNormal"></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">As far as east is from west, so he has removed far from us the guilt of our transgressions. </span></li><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">As a father pities his children, so Yahweh pities those who fear him. </span></li></ul><p></p>
	
	<p class="MsoNormal"><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">For he knows our frame. He remembers that we are dust. </span>
	</p>
	<p class="MsoNormal"></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">As for man, his days are like the grass. As the flower of the field, so he blossoms. </span></li><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">When the wind passes over it, it is no more, and its place knows it no longer. </span></li></ul><p></p>
	
	<p class="MsoNormal"><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">But the loyal love of Yahweh is from everlasting to everlasting on those who fear him, and his righteousness to their children’s children,&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic;">to those who keep his covenant and remember to do his precepts.</span></p>
	<p class="MsoNormal"><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">Yahweh has established his throne in the heavens, and his kingdom rules over all. </span>
	</p>
	<p class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: left;"></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="font-style: italic;">Bless Yahweh, you his angels, you mighty heroes who do his word by obeying the sound of his word.</span></li><li><span style="font-style: italic;">Bless Yahweh, all you his hosts, you his attendants who do his will.</span></li><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">Bless Yahweh, all his works, in all the places of his dominion.</span>&nbsp;</li><li><span lang="en-US" style="font-style: italic;">Bless Yahweh, O my soul.</span>”&nbsp;</li></ul><p></p></blockquote><blockquote style="text-align: left;"><p class="MsoNormal"><b>(<span lang="en-US">Psalm 103</span>, LEB)&nbsp;&nbsp;<br /></b></p></blockquote><div><p class="MsoNormal"><b></b></p>
	
	</div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><h4 style="text-align: left;">Scofield Reference Bible Notes: PSALM 103</h4><div>103:12 Three Hebrew words are trans, forgive, forgiven: kaphar, to cover; nasa, to lift away; salach, to send away (cf. Lev. 16:21, 22), the fundamental O.T. idea of forgiveness being not the remission of penalty, but the separation of the sinner from his sin. Psa. 103:12 expresses this.</div><div><br /></div><div>C. I. Scofield, ed., The Scofield Reference Bible: The Holy Bible Containing the Old and New Testaments (New York; London; Toronto; Melbourne; Bombay: Oxford University Press, 1917), 648–649.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><h4 style="text-align: left;"><b>Word References</b></h4><div><div>[6411] עָוֹן ʿāwōn 233× sin, wickedness, iniquity, often with a focus on the guilt or liability incurred, and the punishment to follow [5771] See guilt; iniquity; sin; wicked, wickedness.</div><div><br /></div><div>William D. Mounce, Mounce’s Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New Testament Words (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan, 2006), 1007.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><div>[6142] סָלַח sālaḥ 46× [Q] to forgive, release, pardon; [N] to be forgiven [5545]</div><div>Q Qal N Niphal</div><div><br /></div><div>William D. Mounce, Mounce’s Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New Testament Words (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan, 2006), 1002.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div>[8324] 1 רָפָא rāpāʾ 69× [Q] to heal; [N] to be healed, be cured; [P] to heal, repair; [Ht] to recover [7495] See cure; heal; restore. Q Qal N Niphal P Piel</div><div><br /></div><div>William D. Mounce, Mounce’s Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New Testament Words (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan, 2006), 1044.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div>[9377] תַּחֲלֻאִים taḥaluʾîm 5× diseases [8463*]</div><div>* the Lexical form listed by Kohlenberger is slightly different from that listed by Strong.</div><div><br /></div><div>William D. Mounce, Mounce’s Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New Testament Words (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan, 2006), 1063.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div>[1453] גְּאוּלִים geʾûlîm 1× redemption, with an implication that the redemption involves recompense [1350*] * the Lexical form listed by Kohlenberger is slightly different from that listed by Strong.</div><div><br /></div><div>William D. Mounce, Mounce’s Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New Testament Words (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan, 2006), 910.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div><br /><br /><br />

<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Shalom&nbsp;<span face="sans-serif" style="background-color: white; color: #202122; font-size: 14px;">שָׁלוֹם</span>: Live Long and Prosper!</span>
<br />

<div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;; font-size: 18pt;">Darrell Wolfe</span>
</div>

Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant | Freelancer | Bible Nerd
<i>*Written withs some editing and research assistance from ChatGPT-4o</i>
<br /><br /><br />
~~~
